leadText: Horn Attack (Japanese: つのでつく Horn Thrust) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation I.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Horn Attack deals damage and has no secondary effect. Horn Attack can be used as the first move of a Pokémon Contest combination , causing Fury Attack and Horn Drill to score double the normal appeal if used in the next turn. It can also be used to finish a combination, with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Leer was used in the previous turn.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad Stad2 A Normal -type attack. A sharp horn is driven hard into the target to inflict damage. G S C An attack using a horn to jab. R S E Jabs the foe with sharp horns. Colo XD Jabs the target with sharp horns. FR LG D P Pt HG SS PBR The foe is jabbed with a sharply pointed horn to inflict damage. B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The target is jabbed with a sharply pointed horn to inflict damage.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B MD T D S Inflicts damage on the target. B S L てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえる MD GtI UNUSED S MD MD RT DX It damages an enemy.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Horn Attack was the first move that was shown to be used in the animated series . It was used by a Nidorino in the first episode . Omanyte can learn this move in Generation I , despite having no visible horn or tusks. This is likely because its evolved form, Omastar , has spikes on its shell.
